name: Zona de Interés Regional Embalse de Orellana y Sierra de Pela — circular desde Orellana la Vieja
region_intro: Touring cycling around Embalse de Orellana features diverse landscapes characterized by the expansive waters of the reservoir, Mediterranean forests, and dehesas. The terrain includes varied profiles, from smoother sections along service roads and irrigation canals to routes with significant elevation gain. This region, often referred to as Extremadura's "inland sea," offers a mix of natural beauty and developed infrastructure for active tourism. Best touring cycling routes around Embalse de Orellana The most popular touring cycling route is Zona de Interés Regional Embalse de Orellana y Sierra de Pela — circular desde Orellana la Vieja, a 34.5 miles (55.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Sierra de Pela and the reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Guadiana Natural Trail: Orellana Reservoir Loop, a moderate 27.0 miles (43.5 km) path. This route follows sections of the Guadiana Natural Path, providing scenic views along the reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Embalse de la Serena and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer — circular from la Puebla de Alcocer, a 45.4 miles (73.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes and past historical sites, often completed in about 5 hours 7 minutes. Touring cycling around Embalse de Orellana is defined by expansive reservoir waters, Mediterranean forests, and dehesas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de Orellana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Embalse de Orellana?
A: The touring cycling routes around Embalse de Orellana offer a mix of challenges. You'll find &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021414907/guadiana-natural-trail-orellana-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;moderate paths&lt;/a&gt;, often following sections of the Guadiana Natural Path, as well as more &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327246907/zona-de-interes-regional-embalse-de-orellana-y-sierra-de-pela-circular-desde-orellana-la-vieja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;difficult routes&lt;/a&gt; with significant elevation gain, such as those offering views of the Sierra de Pela.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling around Embalse de Orellana?
A: Spring and autumn are considered ideal for touring cycling around Embalse de Orellana due to the pleasant temperatures. These seasons offer comfortable conditions to explore the diverse landscapes without the extreme heat of summer.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while cycling around Embalse de Orellana?
A: The region is rich in history. You can encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Cogolludo&lt;/a&gt; over the Guadiana River, and the impressive Castle of Puebla de Alcocer, which offers panoramic views of the reservoir. The town of Orellana la Vieja also features historical sites like the Orellana Palace Castle and the Church of the Immaculate Conception.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The Embalse de Orellana itself is a major natural draw, often called Extremadura's 'inland sea.' You can enjoy views of the vast waters, Mediterranean forests, and dehesas. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; also features impressive rock formations. For birdwatching enthusiasts, the area is a Special Protection Area for Birds (SPA), offering chances to spot species like griffon vultures and black storks.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in the Embalse de Orellana area?
A: While many routes have moderate to difficult ratings, some sections of the Guadiana Natural Path offer smoother profiles, utilizing service roads along irrigation canals and old roads, which can be more suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Esparragosa de Lares&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option with less elevation.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes around Embalse de Orellana?
A: While the region is known for its natural beauty and outdoor activities, specific regulations regarding dogs on cycling routes are not universally posted. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Natura 2000 Network, and to ensure they are well-behaved and do not disturb wildlife or other visitors.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available around Embalse de Orellana?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327246907/zona-de-interes-regional-embalse-de-orellana-y-sierra-de-pela-circular-desde-orellana-la-vieja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zona de Interés Regional Embalse de Orellana y Sierra de Pela — circular desde Orellana la Vieja&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021409446/embalse-de-la-serena-and-castillo-de-puebla-de-alcocer-circular-from-la-puebla-de-alcocer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Serena and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer — circular from la Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on touring cycling routes here?
A: Elevation gain varies significantly across routes. While some paths are relatively flat, others, particularly the more difficult ones, can involve substantial climbing. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31864986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana de la Sierra loop from Navalvillar de Pela&lt;/a&gt; has over 800 meters of elevation gain, offering challenging ascents and rewarding views.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes around Embalse de Orellana?
A: The Embalse de Orellana region is developing its infrastructure for active tourism. While specific parking areas for each route are not always marked, you can typically find parking in the towns and villages near the start points of the routes, such as Orellana la Vieja or Puebla de Alcocer.

Q: Do I need any special permits to cycle around Embalse de Orellana?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for touring cycling on established routes around Embalse de Orellana. However, as the area is part of the Natura 2000 Network and a Special Protection Area for Birds, it's important to stay on marked paths and respect the natural environment to protect its rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay overnight near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, towns like Orellana la Vieja and Puebla de Alcocer, which serve as common starting points or pass-throughs for many routes, offer amenities including places to eat and accommodation options. These towns are well-equipped to cater to visitors exploring the region.

Q: Can I go wild swimming or visit beaches near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely! The Embalse de Orellana is famous for its inland beaches. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Orellana&lt;/a&gt;, also known as 'Costa Dulce,' was the first inland beach in Spain to receive a Blue Flag award. Another tranquil option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3518873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de los Calicantos&lt;/a&gt;. These spots are perfect for a refreshing dip after a ride.
